arts.ttl: Liverpool next five fixtures compared to Arsenal and Chelsea after title rivals falter
arts.sum: Liverpool have enjoyed a good Saturday without kicking a ball after watching title rivals Arsenal and Chelsea drop points.

arts.ttl: Liverpool FINALLY make Trent Alexander-Arnold contract offer as mega £78m deal on table
arts.sum: The Reds hope to keep their howmegrown right-back who has attracted the interest of Real Madrid as his contract ticks down towards the end of the season

arts.ttl: Manchester United offer Marcus Rashford to AC Milan
arts.sum: As the winter transfer window heats up, AC Milan find themselves at the centre of a potentially game-changing opportunity. Despite January typically being a quiet month for major transfers, the Rossoneri might secure a significant addition to their squad in the form of Marcus Rashford, the English striker currently out of favor at Manchester United. The post Manchester United offer Marcus Rashford to AC Milan appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim shapes up as Manchester United’s fall guy but rot runs deeper | Jonathan Wilson
arts.sum: Unbending coach takes his side to Liverpool on Sunday, with attempts to kick club on undermined by a decade of misrule The thought had always been that it couldn’t happen now. It’s just not possible in modern football that a super-club could be relegated. Manchester United may have gone down in 1974 but it’s not going to happen in 2025. Even when Ruben Amorim said that United were in a relegation battle after Monday’s 2-0 defeat by Newcastle, he was making the point to shock. And it’s not going to happen now. United will not be relegated. They probably only need 15 points from the second half of the season to be safe and the financial structure of modern football means there are at least three sides worse than them. Yet it’s significant that Amorim could mention relegation without it sounding entirely absurd, revealing that it feels worth doing the calculation, working out what sort of tally might be necessary for United to survive. What has happened at United since Sir Alex Ferguson left feels like thought experiment made flesh: what would it take for the most successful side in English history to go down? Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Napoli set to make move for Liverpool’s Federico Chiesa
arts.sum: In a surprising turn of events, Napoli are preparing to make an offer for Liverpool's Federico Chiesa. The Italian international, who has struggled to find his footing at Anfield, could be set for a return to Serie A on a six-month loan deal. The post Napoli set to make move for Liverpool’s Federico Chiesa appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Liverpool vs. Manchester United: Arne Slot to beat Ruben Amorim in key Premier League title race fixture
arts.sum: Liverpool should be confident of thrashing rivals Manchester United on Sunday in the Premier League at Anfield and making a statement in the Premier League title race.

arts.ttl: Gazzetta: Zirkzee wants to leave Manchester United
arts.sum: Juventus are intensifying their efforts to sign Joshua Zirkzee from Manchester United as they aim to bolster their squad and secure a Champions League qualification spot. The Dutch striker, who has struggled to find his footing in the Premier League, is a top target for Juventus manager Thiago Motta, who previously worked with Zirkzee at Bologna. The post Gazzetta: Zirkzee wants to leave Manchester United appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Final Score: Will Liverpool worsen Man Utd's troubles?
arts.sum: Final Score pundits Martin Keown and Anita Asante discuss Man Utd's Premier League season so far and how they may set up against Arne Slot's Liverpool.

arts.ttl: Liverpool GREEN LIGHT transfer that DEVASTATES Man Utd
arts.sum: Liverpool are set to come to a loan agreement with Napoli for the transfer of Federico Chiesa to the Serie A side this month. Chiesa’s agent Fali Ramadani held talks with Liverpool last week, with the Reds agreeing to allow the player to go away on loan until the summer with the idea that he will continue at Anfield from next summer.
